cases.question: What is the remote work policy?
cases.resolvedQuery: What is the remote work policy?
cases.reference: Employees may work remotely 2 days per week with manager approval, and they must complete security checklists before each remote day.
cases.answer:
Employees may work remotely up to 2 days per week with manager approval. [Source 1]  
Security checklists must be completed before each remote day. [Source 1]

cases.question: Compare the remote work policy in these documents.
cases.resolvedQuery: Compare the remote work policy in these documents.
cases.reference: There are no material differences between handbook_alpha and handbook_eta. Both allow remote work 2 days per week with manager approval and require a security checklist before each remote day, even though handbook_eta paraphrases the wording.
cases.answer:
Summary:
- Both documents allow up to 2 days of remote work per week with manager approval and require completing a security checklist before each remote day [Source 1][Source 2].

Per document:
- handbook-alpha.pdf:
  - May work remotely 2 days per week with manager approval [Source 1].
  - Security checklists must be completed before each remote day [Source 1].
- handbook-eta.pdf:
  - May work from home up to 2 days each week when a manager approves the arrangement [Source 2].
  - Complete the security checklist before every remote day [Source 2].

Agreements:
- Limit of 2 remote days per week [Source 1][Source 2].
- Manager approval is required [Source 1][Source 2].
- Security checklist must be completed before each remote day [Source 1][Source 2].

Differences:
- No substantive policy differences are evident in the provided excerpts; wording varies slightly (“work remotely” vs. “work from home”) but requirements match [Source 1][Source 2].

Gaps or uncertainty:
- The excerpts do not specify eligibility criteria, equipment/support, reimbursement, working hours/availability expectations, or processes for exceptions or fully remote arrangements.

cases.question: Compare the remote work policy in these documents.
cases.resolvedQuery: Compare the remote work policy in these documents.
cases.reference: Both documents require manager approval and security checklists before each remote day, but handbook_alpha allows 2 remote days per week while handbook_gamma allows 3.
cases.answer:
Summary:
- Both documents require manager approval for remote work and mandate completing security checklists before each remote day [Source 1][Source 2].
- They differ on the number of remote days allowed per week: 2 days (alpha) vs 3 days (gamma) [Source 1][Source 2].

Per document:
- handbook-alpha.pdf:
  - Employees may work remotely 2 days per week with manager approval [Source 1].
  - Security checklists must be completed before each remote day [Source 1].
- handbook-gamma.pdf:
  - Employees may work remotely 3 days per week with manager approval [Source 2].
  - Security checklists must be completed before each remote day [Source 2].

Agreements:
- Manager approval is required for remote work [Source 1][Source 2].
- Security checklists must be completed before each remote day [Source 1][Source 2].

Differences:
- Allowed remote days per week: 2 days (alpha) vs 3 days (gamma) [Source 1][Source 2].

Gaps or uncertainty:
- No evidence provided on eligibility criteria beyond manager approval.
- No details on equipment/expense support, work hours, location restrictions, or exceptions/enforcement of the checklist requirement.

cases.question: Compare the remote work policy in these documents.
cases.resolvedQuery: Compare the remote work policy in these documents.
cases.reference: Both documents allow remote work 2 days per week with manager approval and require security checklists, but handbook_delta adds an eligibility restriction limiting the policy to full-time engineering employees.
cases.answer:
Summary:
- Both policies allow remote work up to 2 days per week with manager approval and require completion of security checklists before each remote day. [Source 1][Source 2]
- The delta handbook limits eligibility to full-time engineering employees, while the alpha handbook does not state such a limitation. [Source 2][Source 1]

Per document:
- handbook-alpha.pdf:
  - Employees may work remotely 2 days per week with manager approval. [Source 1]
  - Security checklists must be completed before each remote day. [Source 1]
- handbook-delta.pdf:
  - Only full-time engineering employees may work remotely 2 days per week with manager approval. [Source 2]
  - Security checklists must be completed before each remote day. [Source 2]

Agreements:
- Remote work is limited to 2 days per week with manager approval in both documents. [Source 1][Source 2]
- Security checklists are required before each remote day in both documents. [Source 1][Source 2]

Differences:
- Eligibility scope: Delta restricts remote work to full-time engineering employees; Alpha does not specify any department or employment-status restriction in the provided excerpt. [Source 2][Source 1]

Gaps or uncertainty:
- The alpha excerpt does not indicate whether other sections impose role- or status-based limits beyond “Employees.” [Source 1]
- The approval process details and the contents/requirements of the security checklists are not described in either excerpt. [Source 1][Source 2]

cases.reference: Both documents allow remote work 2 days per week and require security checklists, but handbook_alpha requires manager approval while handbook_zeta requires director approval.
cases.answer:
Summary:
- Both policies allow remote work up to 2 days per week and require completing security checklists before each remote day [Source 1][Source 2].
- The only concrete difference shown is the approval authority: manager (alpha) vs director (zeta) [Source 1][Source 2].

Per document:
- handbook-alpha.pdf:
  - Up to 2 remote days per week with manager approval [Source 1].
  - Security checklists must be completed before each remote day [Source 1].
- handbook-zeta.pdf:
  - Up to 2 remote days per week with director approval [Source 2].
  - Security checklists must be completed before each remote day [Source 2].

Agreements:
- 2 remote days per week are permitted [Source 1][Source 2].
- Security checklists are required before each remote day [Source 1][Source 2].

Differences:
- Approval authority:
  - Alpha: manager approval required [Source 1].
  - Zeta: director approval required [Source 2].

Gaps or uncertainty:
- The excerpts do not indicate eligibility criteria, location constraints, equipment or expense policies, or details of the approval/checklist processes.

cases.question: Compare the remote work policy in these documents.
cases.resolvedQuery: Compare the remote work policy in these documents.
cases.reference: handbook_alpha and handbook_beta match each other: both allow remote work 2 days per week with manager approval and require security checklists before each remote day. handbook_gamma matches on approval and checklists but allows 3 remote days per week instead of 2.
cases.answer:
Summary:
- All three policies require manager approval and completing a security checklist before each remote day; handbook-alpha and handbook-beta allow 2 remote days/week, while handbook-gamma allows 3 remote days/week [Source 1][Source 2][Source 3].

Per document:
- handbook-alpha: Remote work allowed 2 days/week with manager approval; security checklist required before each remote day [Source 1].
- handbook-beta: Remote work allowed 2 days/week with manager approval; security checklist required before each remote day [Source 2].
- handbook-gamma: Remote work allowed 3 days/week with manager approval; security checklist required before each remote day [Source 3].

Agreements:
- Manager approval is required for remote work in all three documents [Source 1][Source 2][Source 3].
- Security checklists must be completed before each remote day in all three documents [Source 1][Source 2][Source 3].

Differences:
- Maximum remote days per week: handbook-alpha = 2; handbook-beta = 2; handbook-gamma = 3 [Source 1][Source 2][Source 3].
- handbook-alpha and handbook-beta present identical wording in the quoted sections; no differences observed between them in the provided excerpts [Source 1][Source 2].

Gaps or uncertainty:
- It is unclear which maximum (2 vs 3 days/week) is current or authoritative due to the conflicting limits across documents [Source 1][Source 2][Source 3].
